timeline.label: Secured 5,080 B300 GPUs via Compal (~USD 325M); disclosed financing bridge (advance, borrowings, 23rd rights)
Revised FY2026 forecast: revenue ¥33,601M, OP ¥3,635M, parent EPS ¥115.57 (ramp year)
Revenue guide cut 9.9% vs January 2026 forecast on delayed GPU delivery; profit revised up on timing
Cancelled 22nd stock-acquisition rights (¥818M share-based comp benefit)
Disclosed GPU scale ambition (100k–300k GPUs YE2026 aim, non-binding marketing)
AI data-center revenue begins (interim H1 FY2025: AI DC revenue from Sep 2025)
FY2025 consolidated revenue ¥2,942M; pivot from legacy IT toward Neo Cloud platform
Neo Cloud / TAIZA AI data-center strategy articulated in English IR materials

industry.structure: Japanese neo-cloud AI infrastructure is concentrated among a handful of operators competing for hyperscaler and enterprise GPU tenancy; capital intensity and supply-chain access (NVIDIA/ODM partners) are primary entry barriers.
industry.share_shift: Hyperscalers and global ODMs gain share through in-house capacity and scale; regional neo-cloud players like DataSection compete on location, timing, and customer relationships rather than proven cost advantage.
industry.trend: AI training and inference demand is driving a GPU datacenter buildout in Japan; B300/B200 upgrade cycles and financing bridges create execution volatility for smaller operators.
